capabilities.description: Path, stream, byte, synchronous, and asynchronous document flows.
DOC document and DOT binary-template routing, import, native writing, and template FIB semantics.
Main-story paragraphs, runs, tabs, and line, page, and column breaks.
Sections, page size, margins, orientation, columns, and supported section flags.
Default, first-page, and even-page headers and footers.
capabilities.note: Text, fields, links, bookmarks, content controls, and inline pictures are supported within the documented story subset.

capabilities.description: Character formatting, fonts, colors, language, and text effects supported by DOC.
capabilities.note: Common and tested sprm mappings are editable; unsupported Open XML-only effects block legacy output.

capabilities.description: Paragraph styles, alignment, spacing, indents, borders, shading, tabs, and pagination.
capabilities.note: Common and tested paragraph properties map natively; unsupported properties are preflight-blocked.

capabilities.description: Bullets, numbering, list levels, and list overrides.
capabilities.note: The supported classic numbering subset maps editably in both directions.

capabilities.description: Built-in and custom paragraph and character styles.
capabilities.note: Supported style inheritance and formatting are native; unsupported style properties are diagnosed.

capabilities.description: Table styles and conditional table formatting.
capabilities.note: The BIFF-era Word table-style subset is encoded; Open XML-only conditional properties are blocked.

capabilities.description: Tables, rows, cells, merges, widths, borders, shading, and nested tables.
capabilities.note: Supported tables are editable; native writing currently limits nested-table depth and rejects unsupported shapes.

capabilities.description: Bookmarks in document stories and supported table boundaries.
Internal bookmark and absolute external hyperlinks.
capabilities.note: Simple supported display content is native; unsupported hyperlink payloads block output.

capabilities.description: Common simple and complex field instructions and display results.
capabilities.note: Supported fields retain instructions and results; unknown or active fields require an explicit fallback decision.

capabilities.description: Footnote bodies, references, separators, and supported formatting.
Endnote bodies, references, separators, and supported formatting.
Classic comments, authors, ranges, and comment text.
capabilities.note: Readable classic comments are projected. Rich or structurally unsupported comment content remains guarded.

capabilities.description: Tracked insertions, deletions, authors, dates, and revision settings.
capabilities.note: Supported run-level revisions write natively; moves, property revisions, and unsupported nested markup block legacy output.

capabilities.description: Inline content controls containing supported run-level content.
capabilities.note: DOC has no Open XML content-control carrier; supported content is converted to equivalent editable story content.

capabilities.description: Inline raster and metafile pictures.
capabilities.note: Supported encodings write as editable inline DOC pictures. If the native writer rejects a modern picture or effect, PreferVisual and BestEffort create static page images; PreservationOnly can also retain the source carrier.

capabilities.description: Floating pictures, text boxes, AutoShapes, groups, and drawing anchors.
capabilities.note: Binary OfficeArt is detected or preserved. Modern-to-DOC conversion either blocks or creates deterministic static page images after an omission-free render; PreservationOnly can also retain the source carrier.

capabilities.description: Embedded Word charts and chart previews.
capabilities.note: Modern charts render through the shared Drawing chart model. Strict conversion blocks; visual modes create static page images, and PreservationOnly can also retain the source carrier.

capabilities.description: SmartArt diagrams and their visible drawing fallback.
capabilities.note: DOC has no SmartArt model. Strict conversion blocks; visual modes render the diagram into static page images, and PreservationOnly can also retain the source carrier.

capabilities.description: Embedded OLE packages and their preview images.
capabilities.note: Opaque carrier retention is safe for unmodified legacy round trips; editable cross-format conversion is not claimed.

capabilities.description: ActiveX controls and control storages.
capabilities.note: Opaque carrier retention is safe for unmodified legacy round trips; editable cross-format conversion is not claimed.

capabilities.description: VBA project storage and related macro state.
capabilities.note: Opaque carrier retention is safe for unmodified legacy round trips; editable cross-format conversion is not claimed.

capabilities.description: Built-in SummaryInformation and DocumentSummaryInformation properties.
Supported scalar custom document properties.
Open XML custom XML parts.
capabilities.note: Classic DOC has no package-part equivalent; strict conversion blocks and permissive conversion must report the carrier loss.

capabilities.description: Legacy digital-signature streams and storages.
capabilities.note: Opaque carrier retention is safe for unmodified legacy round trips; editable cross-format conversion is not claimed.

capabilities.description: Legacy DOC password-to-open encryption.
capabilities.note: Encrypted DOC detection is safe, but first-party decryption and encryption are not yet complete.

capabilities.description: Unknown records, table-stream ranges, compound streams, and storages.
capabilities.note: Opaque carrier retention is safe for unmodified legacy round trips; editable cross-format conversion is not claimed.

capabilities.description: Hash-verified original source retained beside an allowed lossy conversion or visual fallback.
capabilities.note: Embedding is explicit because the original bytes may contain macros or hidden content. PreservationOnly enables it automatically; callers can recover the verified payload through TryGetCompatibilitySourcePayload.
